content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95304 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

October 3, 1980

Mrs. Helen Kawagoe, City Clerk
City of Carson
701 East Carson
Carson, California 90749

A - 80-10-051

Dear Mrs. Kawagoe:

In response to your request of September 30, 1980, this
letter is to confirm my advice to you concerning the filing
obligations of Mr. Walter Egan's committee.

The facts you presented are as follows:

1. The "Friends of Walter J. "Jake" Egan" committee
(Committee "A") reported payments of $950.00 and $800.00, a
total of $1,750 to the "Carson News Progress" committee
(Committee "C") on Schedule E, Payments and expenditures for
printing of brochures.

2. The "Committee to Elect Kay Calas" (Committee "B")
reported a payment of $1,750 to the "Carson News Progress" committee
(Committee "C") on Schedule E, Payments and expenditures for
printing of brochures.

3. Both Committee "A" and Committee "B" filed "Late
Contribution" reports showing the expenditures to Committee "C"
since the expenditures to Committee "C" were made after the
closing date of the second pre-election statement and before
the election date pursuant to Government Code Section 84214.

4. Committee "C" filed a "Late Contribution" report
showing receipt of the $1,750 from Committee "A" from Committee
"B" for a total of $3,500, pursuant to Government Code Section
84214.

5. Committee "C" reported receipt of $1,750 from each
Committee "A" and Committee "B" as well as payments to printers
for the printing of brochures in the amount of $3,500 on their
post-election campaign statement.

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

18215

Mrs. Helen Kawagoe
Page 2
October 3, 1980

The question you asked was whether or not Committee "A"
and "B" would be required to report on a "Late Contribution"
report the receipt of an in-kind contribution for the brochures
printed, for which they had already reported making the
payments for?

I advised that the term "contribution" is defined by adopted
regulation by the Fair Political Practices Commission as
follows: ...."'Contribution' includes any monetary or non-
monetary payment for which full and adequate consideration is
not made to the donor that is received by or made at the behest
of:..." (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215).

Therefore, since Committee "A" and Committee "B" fully
paid Committee "C" for the printing of their brochures, they
did give Committee "C" "full and adequate consideration" for
printing the brochures and did not receive an in-kind
contribution from Committee "C" which was paid for goods and
services delivered to Committees "A" and "B."

I hope that this information has been helpful, please
feel free to contact my office for any further assistance.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Arriola
Political Reform
Consultant

HPA:jg
